name: Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — round trip from Campanario
name_original: Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — ida y vuelta desde Campanario
region_intro: Hiking around Campanario explores the diverse landscapes of the Extremadura region in Spain. The area is characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and reservoir surroundings, with trails often passing by historical landmarks. The terrain includes agricultural lands, natural areas, and features such as the Embalse de Orellana and the Río Guadalefra. Best hiking trails around Campanario The most popular hiking route is Orellana la Vieja from Playa de las Casetas – circular by the Embalse and Canal de Orellana, a 10.3 miles (16.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Embalse and Canal de Orellana. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — round trip from Campanario, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This trail follows the Río Guadalefra, providing a scenic river valley experience. Local hikers also love the Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Laguna de los Santos, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Hiking around Campanario is defined by river valleys, reservoir areas, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 7 easy, 9 moderate, and 3 difficult routes. The routes in Campanario are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.25 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Campanario's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Campanario?
A: Campanario offers a good variety of hiking options, with a total of 19 trails available. These include 7 easy, 9 moderate, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Campanario?
A: Hiking in Campanario, located in Extremadura, Spain, will take you through diverse landscapes. You'll encounter rolling hills, scenic river valleys, and areas surrounding reservoirs like the Embalse de Orellana. Trails often pass through agricultural lands and natural areas, providing a rich tapestry of scenery.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Campanario?
A: Yes, Campanario has 7 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293570180/dolmen-de-magacela-circular-por-la-laguna-de-los-santos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.6-mile (4.2 km) route that takes about 1 hour 14 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region around Campanario is rich in history. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or admire the old &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared&lt;/a&gt; over the Ortiga river. These sites offer a glimpse into the area's past and often provide panoramic views.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or pass by reservoirs in Campanario?
A: Yes, many trails in Campanario feature water elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293588024/camino-de-piedraescrita-al-rio-guadalefra-round-trip-from-campanario" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra — round trip from Campanario&lt;/a&gt; follows the Río Guadalefra, offering a scenic river valley experience. Other routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101892674/orellana-la-vieja-from-playa-de-las-casetas-circular-by-the-embalse-and-canal-de-orellana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana la Vieja from Playa de las Casetas&lt;/a&gt;, provide views of the Embalse and Canal de Orellana.

Q: Are there circular walks available in Campanario?
A: Yes, circular walks are a popular option in Campanario. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101892674/orellana-la-vieja-from-playa-de-las-casetas-circular-by-the-embalse-and-canal-de-orellana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana la Vieja from Playa de las Casetas&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293570180/dolmen-de-magacela-circular-por-la-laguna-de-los-santos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela — circular por la Laguna de los Santos&lt;/a&gt; also offers a pleasant loop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Campanario?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Campanario, with an average score of 3.25 stars. Hikers often appreciate the varied terrain, the presence of river valleys and reservoir areas, and the historical sites encountered along the way.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on the hikes in Campanario?
A: Yes, you can find several spots offering great views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabra Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated viewpoint, and historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt; also provide panoramic vistas of the La Serena region.

Q: How long do hikes in Campanario typically take?
A: Hike durations in Campanario vary depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293570180/dolmen-de-magacela-circular-por-la-laguna-de-los-santos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela circular&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293588024/camino-de-piedraescrita-al-rio-guadalefra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Piedraescrita al Río Guadalefra&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2 hours 55 minutes, while longer routes can extend to over 4 hours.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Campanario suitable for families?
A: Many of the 7 easy trails in Campanario are suitable for families, offering accessible terrain and shorter distances. These routes provide a great opportunity for children and adults to enjoy the natural beauty and historical sites together.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Campanario?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't always detailed, most rural hiking areas in Spain, including Campanario, typically offer roadside parking or small designated parking areas near popular starting points or villages. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Campanario?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in rural areas of Extremadura, provided they are kept under control, especially around livestock or wildlife. Always ensure to clean up after your dog and respect local regulations, which may vary in protected natural areas.
tips: Nucleus located between Villanueva de la Serena and Castuera, where one of its most characteristic points is the El Pairón Reservoir, located to the west of the same nucleus.
Right next to the Río Guadalefra we find this small hermitage that is located at the top of a hill and from where we can see the entire valley formed by the river with these careful rock structures.
